novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1 / thm:oracle-private-local-rate
novelty_flags.one_line: The oracle theorem is a routine randomized-treatment embedding of the pointwise central-DP regression problem covered by the one-server specialization of CaiChakrabortyVuursteen2024OptimalFederated, not an independent causal novelty kernel.

novelty_flags.label: r_n^CATE benchmark / Proposition 1 / Open-ended Question 1
novelty_flags.one_line: KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE explicitly works with gamma at least beta, whereas the proposal permits arbitrary positive beta and gamma while calling its formula that paper's benchmark.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Impose 0<beta<=gamma and state the exact density-qualified Kennedy et al. comparison regime, or redefine the non-private benchmark for the proposal's larger parameter space.
Recast the randomized oracle result as a cited calibration lemma and make the novel kernel the observational private-nuisance upper/lower frontier.
Split the OEQ into paired upper- and causal-DP-tracing lower-bound conjectures once a candidate third-regime exponent is derived.

recommended_next_step: Continue revision: repair the Kennedy benchmark regime and demote the regression-reduction theorem before advancing the retained observational central-DP CATE frontier.
